entities.name: BUNDELKHAND REGION
entities.description: A region in Central India characterized by degraded landscapes and fragile ecologies where the study was conducted.

entities.name: CENTRAL INDIA
entities.description: The broader geographical area encompassing the study region.

entities.name: RAINWATER MANAGEMENT
entities.description: Interventions used to harvest surface runoff and facilitate groundwater recharge to build resilient agricultural systems.

entities.name: WATER BALANCE COMPONENTS
entities.description: The various elements of the hydrological cycle quantified to assess the impact of rainwater management.

entities.name: IRRIGATION USE
entities.description: The utilization of water for agricultural purposes, impacted by rainwater management interventions.

entities.name: CROP INTENSIFICATION
entities.description: The increase in crop production per unit area, influenced by improved water management.

entities.name: ENERGY CONSUMPTION
entities.description: The amount of energy used in the agricultural system, specifically related to water management and irrigation.

entities.name: SURFACE RUNOFF
entities.description: Water that flows over the land surface, which is harvested by masonry structures during rainwater management.

entities.name: GROUNDWATER RECHARGE
entities.description: The process of water infiltrating the soil to replenish aquifers, facilitated by rainwater management.

entities.name: MONSOON SEASON
entities.description: The period of heavy rainfall during which net groundwater recharge is estimated.

entities.name: DEGRADED LANDSCAPES
entities.description: Land areas with diminished productivity and fragile ecologies requiring management interventions.
